source.title: Cortical VIP neurons locally control the gain but globally control the coherence of gamma band rhythms

confidence_rationale: High-quality mouse V1 study combining electrophysiology, optogenetics, and computational modeling. Directly shows a circuit mechanism by which gamma coherence across cortical regions tracks continuous vs. discontinuous image features -- a plausible substrate for entity-boundary binding. Transfer to human/primate entity emergence and to REE's latent-space binding requires additional mapping steps.

mapping.source_claim_statement: VIP disinhibitory interneurons in mouse V1 linearly scale local gamma power without changing stimulus tuning, but suppress long-range gamma synchronization when two regions process non-matching stimuli -- thereby constructing gamma-band filters for spatially extended but continuous image features such as contours.
mapping.ree_translation: In REE, entity emergence requires that latent representations of co-occurring features cohere into a unified object token. The VIP-gated gamma coherence mechanism provides a candidate neural substrate: gamma synchrony tracks the spatial continuity of a stimulus, encoding entity-boundary information. This maps onto the Q-001 question of what circuit mechanisms bind distributed feature representations into a single entity.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study is entirely in mouse V1 using drifting grating stimuli -- a far simpler scene than the multi-attribute entities REE aims to represent. The gamma-as-binding hypothesis remains contested: the paper itself notes that VIP interneurons are 'constructing filters for contours', not demonstrating a complete binding mechanism. REE entity emergence involves multiple feature dimensions beyond orientation; the generalizability of this VIP circuit to higher-order entity formation is not established.
mapping.source_context: Mouse primary visual cortex, in vivo electrophysiology with optogenetic VIP suppression, idealized grating stimuli.

confidence_components.notes: Excellent methodological quality (Nature-tier journal, optogenetics + modeling). Mapping fidelity is moderate: the study identifies a circuit for spatial coherence gating, which is one component of binding but not the full mechanism of entity emergence. Transfer risk is appreciable because REE entities are abstract multi-attribute objects, not simple orientation gratings.
